events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Field service visit, PPE selection, Safety & Rigging. Observed field condition: voltage glove class wrong or overdue. Work rough-complete. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Voltage glove class wrong or overdue means electrical shock hazard, PPE inadequate. Could be critical if exposed to energized work. Use high maybe critical. Need component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant, worn/degraded? Overdue could be degraded/worn. Wrong class is non-compliant. We'll include installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 75. value 0. confidence 1.0. findings: voltage-rated gloves incorrect class for exposed voltage; gloves past retest date; no compliance with NFPA 70E 130.7(C)(7) and OSHA 1910.137. actions: remove from service, select proper class voltage gloves, send for retest/replace, verify retest date, complete PPE assessment. references: NFPA 70E, OSHA 1910.137, OSHA 1910 Subpart I. Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

    "Voltage-rated gloves on site are the wrong class for the exposed voltage level.",
    "Voltage-rated gloves are past the required retest/expiration date.",
    "Selected PPE does not provide adequate protection for energized electrical work."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove the incorrect or overdue voltage gloves from service.",
    "Select and issue voltage gloves with the correct class rating for the task voltage.",
    "Send existing gloves for required dielectric retest or replace if expired/defective.",
    "Verify glove test dates and condition before next use.",
    "Update the job hazard analysis and PPE assessment before continuing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Voltage-rated gloves are either under-rated for the exposure or overdue for retest, leaving workers unprotected against electric shock during energized work. This is a direct violation of electrical PPE requirements and must be corrected before work proceeds.",

    "NFPA 70E",

  ]
